title: Crispy Peppery Jamaican Pepper Stew
description: A hearty one-pot dish featuring crispy toasts and spicy peppers for a keto-friendly, peppery kick in your meal.

ingredients.name: 1 can (500ml) coconut milk
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed fresh lime juice for dressing

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: or any other healthy fat of your choice

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup (75g) crushed corn tortillas
ingredients.notes: or any other whole grain toast of your choice

ingredients.name: Freshly squeezed fresh lime juice, for serving

instructions: Toast corn tortillas in a pan over medium heat until crispy and lightly golden. Set aside.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onions and garlic; sauté for about 5 minutes until softened.
Add diced tomatoes and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes. Stir in paprika and cayenne pepper. Cook for another minute to meld the spices.
Gently fold in toasted tortillas, allowing them to absorb some of the tomato and spice mixture.
Pour coconut milk into the pot, then stir in salt. Bring the stew to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let it cook for about 15 minutes until flavors have melded together.
Serve warm with freshly squeezed lime juice on top.
